tables.title: Blood Pressure Management in Acute Ischemic Stroke
rows: IV labetalol 10–20 mg over 1–2 min (repeat ×1) OR nicardipine 5 mg/hr (titrate ↑2.5 mg/hr q5–15 min, max 15 mg/hr)
Labetalol or nicardipine as above; reduce BP by ~15% in first 24 h
Must confirm no ICH, no cardiac ischemia, no aortic dissection

tables.title: IV tPA (Alteplase) Dosing and Administration for Stroke
rows: 10% of total dose IV over 1 minute
Remaining 90% of total dose IV over 60 minutes
≤4.5 hours from symptom onset (last known normal)
ICH on CT, active bleeding, stroke <3 months, INR >1.7, platelets <100K, glucose <50, multilobar infarction >1/3 hemisphere

tables.title: Stroke Screening Tools Comparison
rows: Facial droop, arm drift, speech abnormality
Any 1 of 3 abnormal = high probability of stroke
11 items (consciousness, gaze, visual fields, facial palsy, motor, ataxia, sensory, language, dysarthria, extinction)
Quantifies stroke severity (0–42); guides treatment decisions
